name: Intro Kinetic Cascade · editorial phrase cascade with icon climax
one_line: A spoken/anchored line laid out as a sequence of big editorial PHRASES — each a stacked poster with one enlarged accent HERO word — revealed word-by-word on its anchors, hard cut-out between phrases, climaxing on a phrase that SLIDES in with a swappable, ringing ICON (bell / cursor / sparkle / … or any emoji / SVG).

base_template.notes: A narration/anchor-driven kinetic-typography intro. Each PHRASE is a vertically-centered, left-aligned stack of lines (small word / BIG accent hero word / closing words) with per-line size + indent for an editorial diagonal composition. Words reveal one-by-one on their anchors (VO word-times OR audio onsets), the phrase holds, then HARD cuts out before the next. The finale is a CLIMAX phrase that slides in from offscreen-right and HOLDS, carrying a swappable inline ICON that pops + rings. Phrase count, words-per-line, and the icon are all DATA, so any sentence + any scene icon 'just works'. Asset-free: system type, inline-SVG icon library, bundled GSAP.

themes._: Palette presets (the `theme` param). Accents are AA-tuned on each bg. ink = body/closing words; accents[] = hero words (cycled per phrase); 'gradient' = animated sweep between gradFrom/gradTo.
themes.light: editorial light — near-white field, near-black ink, deep green / ocean / sweep accents (DEFAULT; the act0 look)
themes.dark: dark field, off-white ink, vivid green / cyan / sweep accents
themes.bold: deep-navy field, white ink, amber / pink / sweep accents

icons._: The climax ICON is its own knob (`icon`) so it can match the scene. Built-in inline-SVG library (asset-free, fill follows the text color); or pass an emoji, a raw <svg>…</svg> string, or 'none' for a pure-text climax. The pop + ring + wiggle emphasis is generic, so any glyph 'rings'.
icons.examples: bell → timing/alerts · cursor → a click/UI · sparkle → AI/magic · bolt → speed · play → video · check → done · heart → like · star → featured

match.notes: Reach for this for an INTRO / opening statement: a short narrated or anchored line you want to land word-by-word as big editorial type, climaxing on one keyword + icon. Each word reveal lands on one anchor (per_onset_sequential), so it shares the structural_type with the typewriter / logo-sting templates; it is discriminated by laying the line out as a SEQUENCE OF WHOLE PHRASES (big mixed-size posters with a colored hero word, hard-cut between) and by a slide-in ICON climax — not one typed message, not a card deck. ORIGIN is VO-driven (reveal times come from a transcript); in a beat context bind `phrases[].times` to onset seconds instead. If there is no readable line to speak/anchor (pure abstract field) this is the wrong template.

examples.line: If you've EVER used AI / for VIDEO editing before, / you KNOW that / timing is 🔔 tricky.
examples.note: Reversed from the VO intro `act0-intro-bell` (golden_sample_reverse/.../timeline-launch-video). Reveal times are VO WORD onsets (a transcript), not audiomap musical onsets — so the cadence is real but its driver is narration. The original used a Lottie bell + Google-Fonts Inter/Playfair + CDN GSAP; this promoted impl is offline (system type + bundled GSAP) and the bell is an inline-SVG library icon (now swappable per scene). Bind `times` to audio onsets to use it beat-driven.

phrase.notes: Each phrase is a stacked editorial poster, vertically centered. Its words fade in one-by-one on their anchor times (explicit `times`, else auto-spread across the phrase), the phrase holds, then HARD cuts out (opacity→0) at `out`. PROPOSED verb 'word_cascade_reveal' (per-anchor word fade within a held poster). ~typewriter_reveal.

hero.notes: One line per phrase is the HERO — much larger (≈500px) and colored from the theme accent ramp (cycled per phrase), optionally serif-italic ('ever') or an animated 'gradient' sweep ('know'). Carries the key word of each phrase. PROPOSED verb 'accent_blowup'.

climax.notes: The finale phrase SLIDES in from offscreen-right (x 1400→0, power3.out) and HOLDS to the end (it does NOT cut out). Mixed body + one inline icon. PROPOSED verb 'slide_in_lockup'.

icon.notes: A swappable inline glyph in the climax (the {icon} token): pops in (scale 0.4→1, back.out), does a damped ring-wiggle (rotation), and emits two expanding rings. Resolves `icon` to a library SVG, a custom <svg>, an emoji/text glyph, or nothing. PROPOSED verb 'icon_ring'. ~radial_burst_lines for the rings.

field.notes: A flat solid field (theme bg). Dead still; only the type moves. negative_space_hold.

theme.notes: Palette preset: light | dark | bold. Sets bg + ink + the hero accent ramp.

icon.notes: The ringing climax icon — MATCH IT TO THE SCENE. A library name (bell|cursor|sparkle|bolt|play|heart|check|star), an emoji, a raw <svg>…</svg> string, or 'none' (pure-text climax).

phrases.notes: JSON array of phrase objects { lines:[{text,size,x,hero?,accent?,font?}], out, times? }. `times` (per word, reading order) = the VO/onset anchors; omit to auto-spread. Empty = the built-in 3-phrase act0 intro.

climax.notes: JSON object { text, in, iconAt, hold, size }. `text` embeds {icon} where the icon rings. Empty = the built-in 'timing is {icon} tricky.'

engine.model: data phrases + climax + icon + theme. The phrase posters, the climax lockup, the icon library, and the palette themes are fixed code; WHAT they say + WHEN (the anchors) + WHICH icon + WHICH palette are DATA. Word anchors are explicit `times` (VO word-times or audio onsets) or auto-spread across each phrase. Compose by editing the `phrases` / `climax` / `icon` / `theme` DATA — never the engine.
engine.extend: add a palette = a THEMES entry (bg + ink + accents + gradFrom/gradTo); add an icon = an ICONS entry (an inline-SVG path); restyle a phrase via line size/x/hero/accent/font. To rebuild the motion (slide direction, ring count) edit the timeline, not the data.

program_format._: `phrases` is a JSON array; `climax` is a JSON object. Times are absolute seconds (VO word onsets, or audio onsets in a beat context).

phrase.lines: [{ text, size(px), x(indent px), hero?(bool → big accent line), accent?('#hex'|'gradient'), font?('display'|'serif') }]
phrase.out: number — seconds the phrase cuts out
phrase.times: number[]? — per-WORD reveal seconds, reading order across all lines; omit → auto-spread evenly across the phrase
phrase.start: number? — first-word time when `times` omitted (default = previous phrase `out`)

climax.text: string — words + a {icon} token where the icon rings
climax.iconAt: number — when the icon pops + rings
climax.hold: number — tail hold after the ring (sets total duration = iconAt + hold)
climax.size: number — word size px (the icon scales from it)

program_data: program.json (the default act0 phrases + climax as standalone, agent-editable data)
uses_primitives_notes: word_cascade_reveal / accent_blowup / slide_in_lockup / icon_ring are PROPOSED custom phrases the impl defines (per-anchor word fade in a held poster; an enlarged accent hero word; an offscreen slide-in finale; an icon pop + ring-wiggle + expanding rings). typewriter_reveal backs the per-word reveal; content_swap the hard cut-out between phrases; radial_burst_lines the rings; negative_space_hold the static field.
bundled_assets: assets/gsap.min.js (offline GSAP). No fonts bundled — system stack ('Inter','Helvetica Neue',Arial; serif hero falls back Playfair→Georgia). Icons are inline SVG (no Lottie). No remote assets.

provenance.source: act0-intro-bell — the VO intro of the Timeline-Editor launch video (timeline-launch-video).
provenance.note: VO/narration-driven in origin (reveal times = transcript word onsets), NOT a beat-reversed musical group — so the match-face is a reasonable hypothesis for the BGM Director, and reveal times should be bound to audio onsets when used beat-driven. Adapted for the catalog: dropped the remote deps (Lottie bell + Google-Fonts Inter/Playfair + CDN GSAP) for an offline build (system type + bundled GSAP + inline-SVG icons), parameterized the content (phrases/climax) and palette (themes), and made the climax icon a swappable, scene-matched knob. First time it is matched to a real musical group, add that group as an example and tighten requires.
